text: Water-selective capture of carbon dioxide remains a major unsolved issue in the atmosphere. Here, we demonstrate that the interior of the IRMOF-74-III metal–organic framework (MOF) can be covalently post-grafted with primary amines resulting in an upgraded material capable of capturing CO2 under 65% relative humidity (RH). Synthesis, structural analysis and gas adsorption properties of the post-grafted MOFs are presented including as-synthesised samples with various functionalities such as -CH3, -NH2, -CH2NHBoc, -CH2NMeBoc, -CH2NH2, -CH2NHMe functional groups. Cross-polarization magic angle spinning (13)C NMR spectroscopy reveals that the -CH2NH2 and -CH2NHMe functionalities lead to a chemical binding with CO2 through the formation of a carbamate species. Adsorption isotherms, and breakthrough curves show that the IRMOF-74-III-CH2NH2 variant yields a capacity of 3.2 mmol g⁻¹ at 800 Torr and fully meets the request of a wet gas application by capturing CO2 from wet nitrogen over a breakthrough time of 610 ± 10 s g⁻¹ whilst preserving the crystallinity of the material.
explanation: The passage reads like a concise, well-edited scientific abstract with concrete experimental details and measurements; such precise, domain-specific content is more typical of human authors, though advanced AI could replicate it.
